revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Ichio Miyazuka Kofun]][[ja:市尾宮塚古墳]]
{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} | name = Ichio Miyazuka Kofun | native_name =市尾宮塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Ichio Miyazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Ichio Miyazuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ichio Miyazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.44258333333333|N|135.77216666666666|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Takatori, Nara|en|Takatori, Nara|qid=Q1202681|lt=Takatori, Nara|1=Takatori, Nara}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|qid=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|26|33.32|N|135|46|19.83|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Ichio Miyazuka Kofun'''|市尾宮塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Ichiohaka neighborhood of the town of {{ill|Takatori, Nara|en|Takatori, Nara|qid=Q1202681|lt=Takatori|1=Takatori, Nara}}, [[Nara prefecture]] in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|qid=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. Together with the [[Ichiohakayama Kofun]], the tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1981.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/217129|title=市尾墓山古墳・宮塚古墳 |language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ichio Miyazuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''{{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=zenpō-kōen-fun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located at the southern edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|en|Nara Basin|qid=Q10942269|lt=Nara Basin|1=Nara Basin}}, on an independent hill (106 meter elevation) across the from a ridge that extends north from the Ryumon Mountains. A Tenmangu Shrine is currently located on the hill, and Ichiohakayama Kofun is located to the northeast of the hill. Part of the tomb was excavated around 1893, and {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|qid=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} were unearthed. Modern [[archaeological excavation]]s have been conducted since 1975. The tumulus is orientated to the northwest, with a total length of about 44 meters, with a 23 meter wide anterior rectangular portion, and 23 meter diameter posterior circular portion. The mound was built in two tiers. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a double-sided horizontal-hole stone chamber in the center of the circular portion, measuring 11.6 meters in length. It contained a hollowed-out house-shaped Sarcophagus made of {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|qid=Q484924|lt=Tuff|1=Tuff}}, as well as the remains of a wooden coffin from the time of the secondary burial. The exterior of the stone sarcophagus was painted with red pigment. Numerous grave goods, including a silver-decorated dragon-patterned twisted ring-headed iron sword, a gilt bronze-decorated ring-headed iron sword, accessories, including beads and other jewelry, horse equipment, and {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} pottery have been recovered. From these grave goods and the style of the mound, this ''kofun'' was constructed in the first half of the 6th century, during the late Kofun period, shortly after the Ichiohakayama Kofun. As important ruins such as the Mizudoro Kofun and the remains of the Kose temple pagoda are found in the surrounding area, this tumulus has been linked to the Kose clan, a powerful late Kofun period clan who ruled this area during this period and who were active in diplomacy and military affairs with the {{ill|Korea|en|Korea|qid=Q18097|lt=Korea|1=Korea}}. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Currently, the site is open to the public after maintenance work has been completed, but access to the burial chamber is restricted. The tumulus is about a five-minute walk from {{ill|Ichio Station|en|Ichio Station|qid=Q5986275|lt=Ichio Station|1=Ichio Station}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Railway|en|Kintetsu Railway|qid=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu Railway|1=Kintetsu Railway}} {{ill|Yoshino Line|en|Yoshino Line|qid=Q901085|lt=Yoshino Line|1=Yoshino Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|qid=Q17050860|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons category-inline|Ichio Miyazuka Kofun}} *[https://sightseeing2.takatori.info/miyatuka/ Takatori Sightseing Guide]{{in lang|ja}}
{{Kofun navbar}}
